teams.form: Won the 2025 Gold Cup (beating the USA 2-1 in the final) and the Nations League, but closed 2025 on an alarming six-game winless run with only one win in eight against non-CONCACAF opponents. They answered the doubts immediately: a 2-0 win over South Africa (Jimenez header, Quinones) in the tournament's chaotic three-red-card opener at the Estadio Azteca on June 11. Group A continues vs South Kore
teams.key_players: Captain Edson Alvarez leads the side; Raul Jimenez and Julian Quinones powered the opening win; 40-year-old Guillermo Ochoa is included for a record sixth World Cup.
teams.injuries: Right-back Rodrigo Huescas misses out after tearing his ACL in the Champions League in late 2025; several fringe contributors were lost to major injuries in the second half of 2025.
teams.manager: Javier Aguirre, in his third stint in charge of El Tri.
teams.story: After a winless end to 2025 had fans fearing the worst, Aguirre's hosts won the opening match of the entire World Cup at the Azteca — now the perennial 'quinto

teams.form: The only Asian side unbeaten in qualifying, and they are already up and running: a 2-1 comeback win over Czechia in Guadalajara in the tournament's second match (Hwang In-beom scored and assisted, sub Oh Hyeon-gyu got the 80th-minute winner). Hong recently shifted to a back-three system. Next up in Group A: hosts Mexico on June 19.
teams.key_players: Son Heung-min (33, now at LAFC, 4th World Cup, 142 caps) leads the line; Kim Min-jae (Bayern) anchors the defense and Lee Kang-in (PSG) and Hwang In-beom drive the midfield.
teams.injuries: Young winger Bae Jun-ho was an ankle doubt before the opener and a spate of midfield injuries forced the tactical reshuffle to a back three; left-back Lee Tae-seok returned from a calf issue.
teams.manager: Hong Myung-bo, the 2002 semi-final captain, now leading his country at a World Cup.
teams.story: Son's last World Cup dance began with a comeback win — now a date with co-host Mexico in Guadalajara will decide who tops Group A.

teams.form: Survived two penalty shoot-outs (vs Ireland and Denmark) in the March play-offs to reach their first World Cup since 2006; gritty rather than glamorous.
teams.key_players: Tomas Soucek (captain), Patrik Schick
teams.story: Koubek, 74, is the oldest coach in World Cup history

teams.form: Won their CAF qualifying group despite a three-point deduction scare; a settled, youthful Bafana side at their first finals on merit since 2002.
teams.key_players: Lyle Foster (Burnley), Ronwen Williams (captain, GK), Teboho Mokoena
teams.story: First qualification on merit since 2002

teams.form: Topped their European qualifying group unbeaten ahead of Sweden, Kosovo and Slovenia, conceding just two goals in six games, and have lost only to Spain and Germany in the past 18 months. In Group B they face Qatar and Bosnia in Vancouver either side of a June 24 clash with co-host Canada — and fancy themselves to top a group without a giant.
teams.key_players: Captain Granit Xhaka (Sunderland) at his fourth World Cup alongside fellow 2009 U17 world champion Ricardo Rodriguez; Manuel Akanji and Gregor Kobel anchor the defense, Breel Embolo (6 qualifying goals) leads the line, a
teams.injuries: No notable injury concerns reported — Embolo, so often disrupted by injuries, arrives in strong condition and Xhaka is fully fit.
teams.story: The quietly excellent perennial round-of-16/quarter-final team senses a real opening: win a giant-free Group B (including the Canada showdown in Vancouver) and

teams.form: Steadily rising under Jesse Marsch (Copa America 2024 semi-finalists and a string of strong 2025 results), Canada co-host their first men's World Cup and play all three Group B games at home — opening June 12 vs Bosnia and Herzegovina in Toronto before facing Qatar and Switzerland in Vancouver. Final pre-tournament friendlies included a June match vs Uzbekistan.
teams.key_players: Captain Alphonso Davies (Bayern Munich) and striker Jonathan David (Juventus) headline, with Tajon Buchanan (Villarreal) a key attacking piece.
teams.injuries: Davies is doubtful for the opener with a hamstring injury from Bayern's May 6 Champions League semi-final — Marsch is targeting the third group game or the knockouts. Centre-back Moise Bombito was cut on the eve of the t
teams.manager: Jesse Marsch, who named the 26-man roster on May 29, 2026.
teams.story: A home World Cup with all group games on Canadian soil and a genuine shot at a first-ever knockout-round appearance — if they can survive Davies's fitness race

teams.form: Edged through the AFC fourth round to qualify on merit for the first time; two-time Asian champions but unproven at this level away from home.
teams.key_players: Akram Afif, Almoez Ali (all-time top scorer, 60 goals)
teams.story: First World Cup reached on merit after hosting in 2022

teams.form: Won play-off Path A by knocking out Italy in the final (Bajraktarevic converted the decisive penalty) to reach their second-ever World Cup, first since 2014.
teams.key_players: Edin Dzeko (40-year-old captain, 73 international goals), Esmir Bajraktarevic
teams.story: Dzeko's last dance after eliminating Italy

teams.form: Worst-ever qualifying campaign: 5th in CONMEBOL (8W-4D-6L, 28 pts), incl. 1-4 in Buenos Aires and first home WCQ defeat; Ancelotti arrived mid-2025 and steadied things (qualification sealed 1-0 vs Paraguay, Jun 2025). Warm-ups: vs Panama May 31 at Maracana (result unverified), W 2-1 Egypt (Jun 6, Cleveland). Opener Jun 13 vs Morocco.
teams.key_players: Vinicius Jr (Real Madrid, ~16 LaLiga goals but an up-and-down year); Raphinha (Barcelona); Neymar (Santos, fitness gamble, all-time top scorer); Bruno Guimaraes (Newcastle); Alisson (Liverpool GK).
teams.injuries: Neymar (34) included despite a grade-2 calf strain that ruled him out of both warm-up games; availability for the opener uncertain. Otherwise squad broadly fit.
teams.story: Ancelotti tries to turn Brazil's ugliest qualifying campaign into a sixth star, with Neymar's calf the daily soap opera.

teams.form: Crowned AFCON 2025 champions in extraordinary circumstances — the January final in Rabat vs Senegal saw a walk-off protest and was later awarded 3-0 to Morocco by the CAF Appeal Board. Dominant through African qualifying and warmed up with a June 7 friendly against Norway. In Group C with Brazil, Scotland and Haiti, expected to advance behind or alongside Brazil.
teams.key_players: Captain Achraf Hakimi (95 caps, PSG) is the talisman; Brahim Diaz was AFCON 2025 top scorer with 5 goals; Yassine Bounou and Sofyan Amrabat among nine returnees from the historic 2022 semi-final squad.
teams.injuries: No major injuries reported in the final squad (unverified); the bigger disruption was off the pitch with the coaching change.
teams.manager: Mohamed Ouahbi, promoted after leading Morocco to the 2025 U20 World Cup title — he took over when Walid Regragui resigned on March 5, 2026, just three months before the tournament.
teams.story: The 2022 semi-finalists arrive as controversial AFCON holders navigating a late coaching change, with Hakimi leading a bid to prove Africa's best can go even fu

teams.form: Qualified directly with a dramatic 4-2 win over Denmark at Hampden (McTominay bicycle kick, McLean halfway-line lob) for their first finals since 1998.
teams.key_players: Scott McTominay (2024-25 Serie A Footballer of the Year), John McGinn, Andy Robertson
teams.injuries: Billy Gilmour out (knee injury in pre-tournament friendly vs Curacao)
teams.story: First World Cup since 1998 after a Hampden miracle

teams.form: Won their CONCACAF group despite playing all 'home' qualifiers abroad due to the security crisis at home — first World Cup since 1974.
teams.key_players: Johny Placide (captain, GK), Duckens Nazon, Frantzdy Pierrot, Danley Jean Jacques
teams.story: Qualified amid national crisis — a team of hope

teams.form: Inconsistent entering their home World Cup: 2025 Gold Cup runners-up (lost the final 2-1 to Mexico, with Pochettino resting Pulisic that summer), and in 2026 lost three of four friendlies — to Belgium, Portugal and Germany (2-1 on June 6) — with only a 3-2 win over Senegal on May 31. They open Group D tonight, June 12, vs Paraguay at SoFi Stadium, then face Australia and Turkey.
teams.key_players: Christian Pulisic (84 caps) is the focal point and fit after workload management; Weston McKennie, Tyler Adams and Gio Reyna all made the 26-man squad named May 26 — the fifth-youngest US World Cup squad ever, mixing 13
teams.injuries: Johnny Cardoso out after May ankle surgery; Tanner Tessmann and Diego Luna missed the cut amid spring injuries; Adams made the squad but missed nearly three months this season with hamstring/knee issues.
teams.manager: Mauricio Pochettino, hired in late 2024 to deliver at the home tournament.
teams.story: Hosts under real pressure: a talented but erratic team must convert home advantage into the deep run American soccer has waited generations for, starting agains

teams.form: Won play-off Path C with 1-0 wins over Romania and Kosovo; a talented young attacking core makes them a dark horse at their first finals since 2002.
teams.key_players: Arda Guler (fully fit), Kenan Yildiz, Hakan Calhanoglu, Ferdi Kadioglu
teams.story: Back after 24 years with the Guler-Yildiz golden youth

teams.form: Qualified directly under Popovic for a sixth consecutive World Cup; well-organized and hard-working but light on top-end star power.
teams.key_players: Jordan Bos (Feyenoord), Riley McGree, Jackson Irvine, Mat Ryan
teams.story: Sixth straight World Cup, post-golden-generation grit

teams.form: Qualified directly from CONMEBOL on the back of Alfaro's organized, hard-to-beat defensive structure — first World Cup since 2010.
teams.key_players: Miguel Almiron, Julio Enciso, Gustavo Gomez (captain)
teams.story: Back after 16 years, built on defensive steel

teams.form: Won qualifying Group A after a shock 0-2 opening loss in Slovakia (first-ever away WCQ defeat), then five straight wins capped by 6-0 vs Slovakia in Nov 2025. June warm-ups strong: W 4-0 Finland (May 31, Musiala scored on comeback), W 2-1 USA in Chicago (Jun 6, Havertz and Sane). Opener Jun 14 vs Curacao.
teams.key_players: Kimmich (captain, Bayern); Wirtz (first WC; underwhelming debut Liverpool season, ~17 goal contributions in 49 apps); Musiala (Bayern, returning); Havertz; Neuer (40, recalled GK).
teams.injuries: Lennart Karl (18) OUT (thigh muscle tear in training), replaced by Ouedraogo. Neuer (40, surprise return from retirement) missed both warm-ups with a calf issue but expected fit for the opener. Musiala back from the brok
teams.story: Rebuilt around Wirtz and Musiala, Germany aim to bury three straight failed tournaments — with 40-year-old Neuer un-retired for one last ride.

teams.form: Finished second in CONMEBOL qualifying behind only Argentina, conceding a remarkable five goals in 18 matches — the best defensive record in the region. Played a June pre-tournament friendly vs Guatemala (Caicedo rested/not starting). In Group E with Germany, Ivory Coast and Curacao, their defensive solidity makes them a strong bet to advance.
teams.key_players: Moises Caicedo (Chelsea) has matured into one of the world's best midfielders; Willian Pacho (PSG) and Piero Hincapie (Arsenal) form an elite centre-back pairing, with teenager Kendry Paez the creative wildcard.
teams.injuries: No notable injuries reported in the final squad (unverified).
teams.manager: Sebastian Beccacece, in charge since August 2024.
teams.story: The tournament's stingiest defense outside the giants: a young, athletic Ecuador believe they can frustrate Germany for top spot and grind their way to the deep

teams.form: Strong, miserly qualifying campaign won their CAF group, though their AFCON title defense ended in a 2-3 quarter-final loss to Egypt.
teams.key_players: Franck Kessie (captain), Amad Diallo, Simon Adingra, Sebastien Haller
teams.story: 2024 African champions out to prove it on the world stage

teams.form: Went unbeaten through CONCACAF qualifying to become the smallest nation (about 150,000 people) ever to reach a World Cup; faces Germany in Group E.
teams.key_players: Eloy Room (captain, GK), Leandro Bacuna, Juninho Bacuna, Tahith Chong
teams.story: Smallest nation ever at a World Cup, with 78-year-old Advocaat

teams.form: Unbeaten in qualifying Group G (6W-2D, 27 scored, 4 conceded), finishing 3 pts above Poland with top spot only sealed on the final matchday. June warm-ups shaky: L 0-1 Algeria (Jun 4, Koeman called it a 'wake-up call'), W 2-1 Uzbekistan (Jun 8, Gakpo two penalties incl. 98th-min winner). Opener Jun 14 vs Japan.
teams.key_players: Van Dijk (captain, Liverpool); Gakpo (Liverpool, on penalties); Frenkie de Jong (Barcelona); Reijnders (mixed first Man City season, 7g/7a); Depay (talisman, fitness doubt); Dumfries.
teams.injuries: Hard hit: Xavi Simons OUT (ACL rupture ~47 days before tournament); De Vrij and De Ligt OUT injured; Jurrien Timber withdrew Jun 8 (replaced by Geertruida); Frimpong left out by choice. Depay (record 55 intl goals) inclu
teams.story: An injury-ravaged defence and a stuttering attack: the eternal nearly-men must find form fast or another Dutch World Cup ends early.

teams.form: Were the first non-host nation to qualify (March 2025) after dominating Asian qualifying, and arrive billed as a dark horse with arguably their best-ever squad, aiming to finally break the round-of-16 ceiling. Group F opens vs the Netherlands on June 14 in Dallas, then Tunisia (Monterrey) and Sweden (Dallas).
teams.key_players: Takefusa Kubo (Real Sociedad) is the world-class attacking spark; Daichi Kamada, Ao Tanaka and new captain Ko Itakura form the spine, Takehiro Tomiyasu is back from long-term injury, and Yuto Nagatomo becomes the first A
teams.injuries: Major blows: Kaoru Mitoma (hamstring, Brighton) was ruled out of the tournament, and captain Wataru Endo (Liverpool) was dropped just days before the opener after slow recovery from a left-foot injury, with Itakura takin
teams.manager: Hajime Moriyasu, in his second World Cup cycle in charge.
teams.story: A golden squad hit by cruel timing — losing Mitoma and then captain Endo on the eve of the tournament — still believes it can beat the Netherlands and make Japa

teams.form: Awful in the group stage but rescued by Nations League play-off route, where Potter's side beat Ukraine away and Poland 3-2 with Gyokeres scoring four goals in two games.
teams.key_players: Viktor Gyokeres (Arsenal, red-hot), Alexander Isak, Lucas Bergvall, Anthony Elanga
teams.injuries: Isak short of full fitness entering the tournament
teams.story: Potter's great escape via the play-offs

teams.form: Qualified early with an iron defense (unbeaten in CAF qualifying), but a round-of-16 AFCON exit cost Trabelsi his job — Lamouchi took over in January 2026.
teams.key_players: Hannibal Mejbri (wears the No. 10), Aissa Laidouni
teams.story: New-coach bounce; chasing a first-ever knockout berth

teams.form: Unbeaten in UEFA qualifying Group J (5W 3D, 29 scored, 7 conceded), extending an unbeaten World Cup qualifying run dating to 2009; highlights included a wild 4-3 win over Wales sealed by a De Bruyne winner. Beat the USA in a spring 2026 friendly. Drawn into a soft Group G with Egypt, Iran and New Zealand and widely expected to reach the knockouts.
teams.key_players: Kevin De Bruyne (34, Napoli) operating as the No. 10 in his expected final World Cup; Youri Tielemans wears the captain's armband; Thibaut Courtois in goal and Romelu Lukaku up front. A younger wave (Doku generation) ble
teams.injuries: Lukaku included as a gamble despite an injury-wrecked season at Napoli; De Bruyne is fit but only returned in March from October surgery. Qualifying top scorer Malick Fofana was left out (selection call, not injury).
teams.manager: Rudi Garcia, who named his 26-man squad on May 15, 2026, keeping faith with the golden-generation veterans.
teams.story: One last shot for the remnants of the most talented Belgian generation that never won the big one — De Bruyne, Courtois and Lukaku chasing redemption through a

teams.form: Qualified early and comfortably from AFC third round for a fourth straight World Cup, but still seeking a first-ever knockout-stage appearance.
teams.key_players: Mehdi Taremi (captain), Sardar Azmoun, Mohammad Mohebi
teams.story: Fourth straight finals amid political tensions playing in the USA

teams.form: Dominant unbeaten CAF qualifying campaign and AFCON 2025 semi-finalists (knocked out holders Ivory Coast en route); arguably Africa's most in-form side alongside Morocco.
teams.key_players: Mohamed Salah (captain, recovered from hamstring tear and fit), Omar Marmoush
teams.injuries: Salah fully recovered; no other major concerns
teams.story: Salah's last realistic shot at World Cup glory

teams.form: Cruised unbeaten through OFC qualifying (beat New Caledonia 3-0 in the final) for a first World Cup since 2010, still chasing a first-ever finals win... beyond their 2010 unbeaten group exit.
teams.key_players: Chris Wood (Nottingham Forest, all-time NZ top scorer), Matt Garbett, Marko Stamenic
teams.story: Wood-led All Whites back after 16 years

teams.form: Euro 2024 champions; won qualifying Group E unbeaten 5W-1D (21 scored, 2 conceded, incl. 6-0 away in Turkey); lost 2025 Nations League final to Portugal on penalties. June 2026 warm-ups: D 1-1 Iraq (Jun 4), W 3-1 Peru (Jun 8, Oyarzabal brace).
teams.key_players: Lamine Yamal (18, Barcelona: LaLiga Player of the Season, 16 goals/11 assists, but injury-shadowed); Pedri (Barcelona); Nico Williams (Athletic); Oyarzabal (Real Sociedad, in scoring form); Unai Simon (GK).
teams.injuries: Lamine Yamal (hamstring, injured Apr 22) and Nico Williams expected fit for the Jun 15 opener vs Cape Verde per De la Fuente, but neither played the warm-ups fully. Fermin Lopez OUT (fractured metatarsal, surgery). Squad
teams.story: European champions and many bookmakers' favourites — but everything hinges on Yamal's hamstring holding up.

teams.form: Qualified via CONMEBOL with 28 points, finishing in the pack just behind Argentina/Ecuador alongside Colombia and Brazil, and entered the tournament ranked around No. 17. Bielsa has instilled a high-pressing, vertical 4-2-3-1 identity. In Group H, the route looks clear until a final group clash with Spain (June 26, Guadalajara) after Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde.
teams.key_players: Federico Valverde (Real Madrid) is the on-field leader, with Ronald Araujo anchoring the defense; Darwin Nunez, Manuel Ugarte, Rodrigo Bentancur and De Arrascaeta key contributors. Fernando Muslera returned from retireme
teams.injuries: No major injury absences reported (unverified); the headline omission was Luis Suarez, left out by Bielsa and missing his first World Cup since 2010.
teams.manager: Marcelo Bielsa, who has overseen a generational and tactical reset of La Celeste since 2023.
teams.story: Post-Suarez/Cavani Uruguay fully belongs to the Valverde generation — Bielsa's intense, modernized La Celeste eye a deep run, with the Spain finale likely decid

teams.form: In turmoil: sacked Herve Renard in April 2026 after heavy friendly defeats (0-4 Egypt, 1-2 Serbia); Donis leads an almost entirely domestic-league squad in a tough group with Spain and Uruguay.
teams.key_players: Salem Al-Dawsari (captain), Saud Abdulhamid
teams.story: Coaching chaos weeks before kickoff

teams.form: Stunned Cameroon to win CAF Group D outright and qualify for a first-ever World Cup — one of the smallest nations ever to do so.
teams.key_players: Dailon Livramento (scored the qualification-clinching goals), Ryan Mendes
teams.story: Tiny island debutants — the tournament's great underdog story

teams.form: Won UEFA qualifying Group D 5W-1D (16 scored, 4 conceded), sealed with 4-0 vs Ukraine in Nov 2025. June 2026 warm-ups mixed: L 1-2 to Ivory Coast in Nantes (Jun 4), W 3-1 vs Northern Ireland (Jun 8, Olise hat-trick).
teams.key_players: Mbappe (Real Madrid: 2025-26 CL top scorer with 15 goals, ~25 LaLiga goals); Dembele (PSG, 2025 Ballon d'Or winner); Olise (Bayern, hat-trick in final warm-up); Thuram (Inter); Doue and Barcola (PSG).
teams.injuries: Mbappe included despite a thigh injury that cost him Real Madrid's LaLiga run-in; expected to captain. Camavinga and Kolo Muani left out of the 26. No other major absences reported.
teams.story: Deschamps' last dance: the 1998 winner-as-player seeks a farewell third star before handing over (Zidane widely tipped to succeed).

teams.form: Arguably Africa's best side: topped their African qualifying group unbeaten in 10 matches (24 points) and famously beat England 3-1 in 2025 — the first African team ever to beat the Three Lions. Coach Thiaw has won 22 of 27 games in charge. But they arrive wounded: the AFCON final 'title' they won on the pitch in January was stripped and awarded 3-0 to Morocco after their walk-off protest, with a
teams.key_players: Sadio Mane (34, Al Nassr) plays his final World Cup before international retirement; captain Kalidou Koulibaly marshals the defense, with Edouard Mendy in goal and Nicolas Jackson (Bayern), Iliman Ndiaye and Ismaila Sarr
teams.injuries: No major injuries reported when the squad was trimmed from 28 to 26 (unverified).
teams.manager: Pape Thiaw, permanent boss since late 2024 — he instigated the AFCON final walk-off.
teams.story: Fueled by a burning sense of injustice over the stripped AFCON title, Mane's farewell tour opens against France at MetLife on June 16 in a loaded Group I with N

teams.form: Europe's form team: a perfect 8-for-8 qualifying campaign (only the sixth European side ever to do it), scoring 37 goals — capped by a 4-1 demolition of Italy at San Siro in November 2025 — to reach a first World Cup since 1998. Warmed up with a June 7 friendly vs Morocco in New Jersey. Group I: Iraq (June 16), Senegal (June 22/23), France (June 26).
teams.key_players: Erling Haaland (25) arrives at his first major tournament as Norway's all-time top scorer (55 goals) after a record 16 goals in qualifying; captain Martin Odegaard is the creative hub, with Sorloth, Strand Larsen, Bobb a
teams.injuries: Haaland is fit; the main concern is Odegaard, whose club season was plagued by fitness problems and who missed the March friendlies.
teams.manager: Stale Solbakken, who announced the squad on May 21 in a video address featuring King Harald V.
teams.story: After 28 years away, Haaland's golden generation storms in off a perfect qualifying campaign — manageable openers vs Iraq and Senegal before a heavyweight group

teams.form: Won the inter-confederation play-off in March, beating Bolivia with Ali Al-Hamadi's opener, to reach a first World Cup since 1986; drawn into a brutal group with France, Senegal and Norway.
teams.key_players: Ali Al-Hamadi, Aymen Hussein
teams.story: First finals since 1986 — a footballing fairy tale

teams.form: Defending champions; topped CONMEBOL qualifying comfortably (clinched first, incl. 4-1 over Brazil in Mar 2025; rare blip 0-1 at Ecuador in Sep 2025). June 2026 warm-ups: W 2-0 Honduras (Jun 6), W 3-0 Iceland (Jun 9, Messi penalty). 17 of the 26 were 2022 winners.
teams.key_players: Messi (38, Inter Miami captain, fit again, last World Cup); Julian Alvarez (Atletico, ~8 LaLiga goals 2025-26, 10 in CL); Lautaro Martinez (Inter); Mac Allister (Liverpool); Emiliano Martinez (Aston Villa GK).
teams.injuries: Balerdi (muscle) ruled out in camp, replaced by Senesi. Messi had a thigh scare with Inter Miami (May 24) but is back training and will play. Romero (knee ligament), Molina and Montiel (muscle) all managing fitness — Sca
teams.story: Messi's farewell tour: Argentina chase the first back-to-back World Cup titles since Brazil 1962.

teams.form: Topped their UEFA qualifying group with six wins in eight to end a 28-year World Cup absence, playing Rangnick's aggressive pressing game.
teams.key_players: Marcel Sabitzer, David Alaba, Marko Arnautovic (8 goals in qualifying), Christoph Baumgartner
teams.story: Golden generation finally reaches a World Cup after 28 years

teams.form: Won their CAF qualifying group comfortably to end a 12-year World Cup absence, with veteran Mahrez in inspired creative form.
teams.key_players: Riyad Mahrez (35), Amine Gouiri, Mohamed Amoura
teams.story: Mahrez's farewell tour, back after 12 years

teams.form: Shock direct qualifiers from the AFC third round, building on their 2023 Asian Cup final run; compact, counter-attacking debutants.
teams.key_players: Musa Al-Taamari (Rennes winger, 23 international goals), Yazan Al-Naimat
teams.story: Debutants riding Asian Cup runners-up momentum

teams.form: 2025 Nations League champions (beat Spain on pens in final). Won qualifying Group F despite a shock 0-2 loss in Ireland, sealing top spot with a 9-1 demolition of Armenia (Bruno Fernandes and Joao Neves hat-tricks). June warm-ups: W 2-1 Chile (Jun 6), W 2-1 Nigeria (Jun 10).
teams.key_players: Ronaldo (41, record 6th World Cup, 143 intl goals); Vitinha (PSG, 3rd in 2025 Ballon d'Or); Bernardo Silva (Man City); Nuno Mendes and Joao Neves (PSG); Bruno Fernandes.
teams.injuries: Ronaldo missed the March friendlies with a hamstring strain but is fit and expected to start. Martinez took 27 players to camp (only 26 registered). Omitted: Palhinha, Antonio Silva.
teams.story: Ronaldo's sixth and surely final World Cup: a Nations League-winning golden generation tries to deliver the one trophy he lacks.

teams.form: Back at the World Cup after missing Qatar 2022, having finished third in CONMEBOL qualifying with 28 points, ahead of Uruguay and Brazil on goal difference. Long unbeaten stretches under Lorenzo (2024 Copa America finalists) and a winning June 8 send-off friendly over Jordan with James starring. In Group K with Portugal, Uzbekistan and DR Congo — strong favorites to advance.
teams.key_players: Luis Diaz arrives in career-best form after winning the Bundesliga in his first Bayern Munich season and was Colombia's qualifying top scorer; captain James Rodriguez (34) remains the creative heart, supported by Richard
teams.injuries: No notable injuries reported in the final 26 (unverified) — Lorenzo kept the core of the qualifying squad intact.
teams.manager: Nestor Lorenzo, who named his 26-man roster on May 25, 2026.
teams.story: An emotional return after eight years away: James's farewell World Cup and Diaz at his peak, with a favorable group setting up a Miami showdown with Portugal fo

teams.form: Won the CAF play-off (beating Nigeria on penalties) then the inter-confederation Pathway 1 in March to reach a first World Cup since 1974 as Zaire; organized and physically strong.
teams.key_players: Yoane Wissa, Cedric Bakambu, Aaron Wan-Bissaka, Chancel Mbemba
teams.story: First finals since Zaire 1974; faces Portugal in Group K

teams.form: First Central Asian nation ever to qualify; results mixed since Cannavaro took over in October 2025 after qualification was sealed.
teams.key_players: Abdukodir Khusanov (Manchester City), Eldor Shomurodov, Abbosbek Fayzullaev
teams.story: Historic World Cup debut for Central Asia

teams.form: Perfect 100% qualifying record under Tuchel (won all 8 in Group K). Earlier 2026 friendlies reportedly D 1-1 Uruguay, L 0-1 Japan (unverified order/window). June warm-ups: W 1-0 New Zealand in Tampa (Kane header), W 3-0 Costa Rica in Orlando (Rice, Gordon pen, Watkins) on Jun 10. Opener Jun 17 vs Croatia.
teams.key_players: Kane (captain, 3rd WC; 14 CL goals for Bayern 2025-26, one behind Mbappe); Bellingham (Real Madrid); Saka (Arsenal); Rice (Arsenal); Gordon (Newcastle, lively in warm-ups).
teams.injuries: No major injuries in final 26: Stones declared fit; Reece James and Spence just back from hamstring/jaw issues. Bigger news is omissions — Cole Palmer (injury-disrupted season), Foden and Alexander-Arnold all left out; T
teams.story: Tuchel's ruthless, big-name-dropping England chase a first World Cup since 1966 with a flawless qualifying record behind them.

teams.form: Cruised through European qualifying unbeaten (7W 1D, 26 scored, only 4 conceded), their first unbeaten cycle since 2006. Runners-up in 2018 and third in 2022, so the bar remains high. Already arrived in the US ahead of a Group L campaign against England, Panama and Ghana.
teams.key_players: Luka Modric (40, AC Milan) captains at a record-equalling sixth World Cup with 196 caps; Josko Gvardiol and Mateo Kovacic anchor the spine, with Perisic, Kramaric and Livakovic adding deep tournament experience (four pla
teams.injuries: Gvardiol and Kovacic both recovered from injury in time, though Gvardiol's fitness was a race after January shinbone surgery (some reports listed him as a doubt). Main weakness is the lack of a proven striker rather than
teams.manager: Zlatko Dalic, in charge since 2017 and architect of the 2018 and 2022 medal runs.
teams.story: Modric's sixth and surely final World Cup: an ageing but battle-hardened core opens against England in Dallas on June 17, aiming to keep Croatia's improbable me

teams.form: Won their CONCACAF final-round group to qualify directly for a second-ever World Cup; well-drilled and tactically mature under Christiansen.
teams.key_players: Adalberto Carrasquilla (2023 Gold Cup Golden Ball), Cecilio Waterman
teams.story: Golden era seeking a first-ever World Cup win

teams.form: Topped their CAF qualifying group to reach the World Cup, but embarrassingly missed AFCON 2025 entirely (winless, bottom of qualifying group) — wildly inconsistent.
teams.key_players: Antoine Semenyo (in career-best form), Jordan Ayew, Thomas Partey
teams.injuries: Mohammed Kudus and Mohammed Salisu both ruled out (long-term injuries)
teams.story: World Cup redemption after AFCON humiliation, without Kudus
